preview_text:
The model favors Czech Republic at 53%, with South Africa facing a steeper path at 18%. The gap of roughly 35 percentage points reflects a clear hierarchical assessment, though the underdog retains a meaningful chance in a knockout context or as part of a longer tournament.

The draw emerges as the second-likeliest outcome at 29%, suggesting the match could remain competitive through regular time. The distribution points toward a moderately open contest rather than a heavily one-sided affair: Czech Republic's majority sits well short of the 60%+ threshold associated with dominant favorites, leaving room for South Africa to influence play and potentially force a decision by other means.

Model and market align closely. Sportsbook consensus places Czech Republic at 54% (within 1 percentage point), draw at 26% (a 3-point difference), and South Africa at 20% (also 2 points off). Polymarket pricing tracks the books almost exactly. These tight margins indicate confidence across sources in the underlying match structure.
